design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate levels by platforming while solving physics-based puzzles involving object manipulation, carrying items to specific locations, and using environmental objects strategically
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing platforming skill execution with puzzle-solving requiring observation and object-property understanding; navigating slopes that scatter carried stacks creates moment-to-moment decision friction
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Exploring alien worlds as Puggsy, an orange space-hopper alien, using ingenuity and object manipulation to overcome obstacles and recover a stolen spaceship
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The game deliberately fuses standard platformer mechanics with physics-based puzzle design rather than action-puzzle separation. Jon Burton's vision was a 'Super Mario World-type game but with puzzles based on physical objects with different properties such as buoyancy, weight, friction.' This is philosophically distinct from pure platformers or pure puzzle games—objects have weight, can topple on slopes, scatter when unbalanced, and must be understood as physical systems. The design rejects pure trial-and-error in favor of observable physical laws. Training levels were added late to bridge the gap, suggesting the core design is 'too complicated for novice players' but the developers stood by the physics-first approach.

difficulty.lives_system: Health-based with power-ups providing temporary invincibility or additional hits; certain objects grant extra lives (hearts)
difficulty.continues_system: Password system allows progression without needing continuous play sessions
difficulty.checkpointing: Password-based progression system; 6 secret levels accessible only via special password obtained from math equations in credits
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Training levels (5 total) available from 'Junior' option provide optional introduction; not all 57 levels or bosses required to reach normal ending, allowing players to skip harder content; secret endings reward skilled players with specific achievements

progression.upgrade_system: Power-up objects scattered throughout levels (invincibility, speed boost, protective shields allowing 1-2 hits); limited-ammunition weapons available but not refillable
progression.unlock_mechanism: 57 total levels (16 secret) with some secret areas requiring password codes derived from end-credits math equations; 51 normal levels must be completed for one secret ending
replayability_hooks: 6 secret endings unlocked by different goals: collecting 'HEROS' blocks, completing all 51 normal levels, playing 6 special secret levels, solving end-credits math equations
Score values differ based on which objects are carried out of level exits
Multiple secret areas and hidden content reward exploration

world_building_techniques: Level theming with varied settings and visual environments
Environmental storytelling through objects (shells, weights, matches, keys, balloons, cups, barrels, candles suggesting particular locations and activities)
Character-driven premise: Puggsy as alien protagonist with clear motivation (recover stolen spaceship)
Boss encounters tied to planet inhabitants (raccoon natives as antagonists)
Secret cinematic sequences/endings as narrative payoffs

boss_design.philosophy: 6 boss characters in standard version (9 in Mega-CD version); bosses are optional—not all must be defeated to complete game, suggesting they are climactic encounters rather than mandatory gatekeeps

faction_indicators: Raccoon natives as planet inhabitants and antagonists
Puggsy as alien outsider/protagonist seeking to recover property
danger_signals: Physics-based hazards (slopes causing item scatter, requiring careful navigation)
environmental_storytelling: Level names reference other works (e.g., 'Wabbits World' references Wiz 'n' Liz, suggesting cultural context)
Object variety implies diverse environments: shells, weights, matches, keys, balloons, cups, barrels, clue-giving chests, knives, candles each suggest different ecological or cultural spaces
